Class Graph

java.lang.Object
evacuation.graph.Graph
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Graph extends Object implements Serializable
Represents the evacuation graph containing nodes and edges. Provides methods for adding/removing elements, querying neighbors, finding paths, and adding random elements.
See Also:
  • Serialized Form

    Constructors

    Description
    Graph()
    Constructs an empty Graph.

  • Constructor Details

    • Graph

      public Graph()
      Constructs an empty Graph.

  • Method Details

    • addNode

      public void addNode(Node n)
      Adds a node to the graph.
      Parameters:
      n - the node to add

    • removeNode

      public void removeNode(Node n)
      Removes a node and all its connected edges from the graph.
      Parameters:
      n - the node to remove

    • getNode

      public Node getNode(String id)
      Finds a node by its unique identifier.
      Parameters:
      id - the node ID to search for
      Returns:
      the node, or null if not found

    • addEdge

      public void addEdge(Edge e)
      Adds an edge to the graph.
      Parameters:
      e - the edge to add

    • removeEdge

      public void removeEdge(Edge e)
      Removes an edge from the graph.
      Parameters:
      e - the edge to remove

    • getEdgesOfNode

      public List<Edge> getEdgesOfNode(Node n)
      Returns all edges connected to the given node (both source and destination).
      Parameters:
      n - the node
      Returns:
      list of edges connected to n

    • getRawNeighbors

      public List<Node> getRawNeighbors(Node n)
      Returns all neighbors of a node, including via blocked edges (raw view). Does not filter blocked nodes or unavailable edges.
      Parameters:
      n - the node
      Returns:
      list of all directly connected nodes

    • getNeighbors

      public List<Node> getNeighbors(Node n)
      Returns the accessible neighbors of a node. Filters out unavailable edges, blocked nodes, and respects edge direction. For directed edges, only traversal from source→destination is allowed.
      Parameters:
      n - the node
      Returns:
      list of accessible neighbor nodes

    • getWeight

      public double getWeight(Node u, Node v)
      Returns the effective weight of the edge between two nodes. Uses the edge's Edge.getWeight() method which accounts for speed modifier.
      Parameters:
      u - source node
      v - destination node
      Returns:
      effective weight, or positive infinity if no edge exists

    • getEdge

      public Edge getEdge(Node u, Node v)
      Finds the edge between two nodes (in either direction for undirected edges).
      Parameters:
      u - first node
      v - second node
      Returns:
      the edge, or null if none exists

    • edgeExists

      public boolean edgeExists(Node a, Node b)
      Checks whether an edge exists between two nodes.
      Parameters:
      a - first node
      b - second node
      Returns:
      true if an edge exists

    • isTooClose

      public boolean isTooClose(double x, double y, double radius)
      Checks whether any existing node is within the given radius of (x, y).
      Parameters:
      x - x coordinate
      y - y coordinate
      radius - minimum distance threshold
      Returns:
      true if a node is too close

    • getClosestNode

      public Node getClosestNode(Node reference, Node excluded)
      Returns the closest non-blocked node to the reference node, excluding the given node.
      Parameters:
      reference - the reference node
      excluded - the node to exclude (may be null)
      Returns:
      closest node, or null if none available

    • generateUniqueId

      public String generateUniqueId(String prefix)
      Generates a unique node ID with the given prefix.
      Parameters:
      prefix - the ID prefix (e.g., "N", "S", "Z")
      Returns:
      unique ID string

    • addRandomNodes

      public List<Node> addRandomNodes(int count, double maxX, double maxY)
      Adds a specified number of random nodes with unique IDs and positions. Nodes are placed within [0, maxX] x [0, maxY], avoiding positions that are too close to existing nodes (minimum distance 0.7).
      Parameters:
      count - the number of nodes to add
      maxX - maximum x coordinate
      maxY - maximum y coordinate
      Returns:
      list of the newly added nodes

    • addRandomEdges

      public void addRandomEdges(List<Node> newNodes, int edgesPerNode)
      Connects new nodes randomly to the existing graph. Each new node gets at most edgesPerNode edges connecting it to random existing (non-new) nodes in the graph, if they are not already connected.
      Parameters:
      newNodes - the list of newly added nodes
      edgesPerNode - maximum number of edges per new node

    • resetStats

      public void resetStats()
      Resets statistics for all nodes and edges in the graph.

    • getNodes

      public List<Node> getNodes()
      Returns:
      the list of all nodes in the graph

    • getEdges

      public List<Edge> getEdges()
      Returns:
      the list of all edges in the graph

      Deprecated.
      Use addNode(Node)

      Deprecated.

      @Deprecated public Node getNoeudLePlusProche(Node ref, Node excl)
      Deprecated.
      Use getClosestNode(Node, Node)

      @Deprecated public String genererIdUnique(String prefix)
